# mailgun_api_keys (Data Source)

Lists Mailgun API keys with optional filtering.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List all API keys
data "mailgun_api_keys" "all" {}

output "api_key_descriptions" {
  value = [for k in data.mailgun_api_keys.all.items : k.description]
}

# List API keys with filtering
data "mailgun_api_keys" "sending_keys" {
  role = "sending"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `domain_name` (String) Filter API keys by domain name.
- `kind` (String) Filter API keys by kind (domain, user, or web).

### Read-Only

- `keys` (Attributes List) List of API keys. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--keys))
- `total_count` (Number) The total number of API keys returned.

<a id="nestedatt--keys"></a>
### Nested Schema for `keys`

Read-Only:

- `created_at` (String) The timestamp when this API key was created.
- `description` (String) The description of this API key.
- `disabled_reason` (String) The reason this API key was disabled.
- `domain_name` (String) The domain this API key is scoped to.
- `expires_at` (String) The timestamp when this API key expires.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier for this API key.
- `is_disabled` (Boolean) Whether this API key is disabled.
- `kind` (String) The type of API key.
- `requestor` (String) The entity that requested this API key.
- `role` (String) The role of this API key.
- `updated_at` (String) The timestamp when this API key was last updated.
- `user_name` (String) The username associated with this API key.
